title: open_meta recognized conventions v2
description: The open (caller-defined) frontmatter tier is generally free-form: additionalProperties is true, so any key is allowed and stored. This schema documents the keys temper *recognizes* and constrains their shape. A recognized key with a wrong shape is a bug — for the FTS-indexed keys it is stored-but-not-indexed (silent search miss), which is exactly what this schema exists to catch. Shapes for tags/relates_to/depends_on/references/preceded_by/derived_from intentionally mirror base.schema.json's open-tier keys; keep them in sync. The indexed set is versioned by migration (v1: keywords@C + descriptor@D, 20260711000040; v2: tags@C).

keywords.description: FTS-indexed at weight C (convention v1). Deliberately-attached topical tags that boost search ranking. A JSON array of strings (space-joined into the vector) or a bare JSON string. Synonymous with `tags` for ranking.

tags.description: FTS-indexed at weight C (convention v2). The everyday topical-tag key; ranks identically to `keywords`, and accepts the same shapes: a JSON array of strings (space-joined into the vector) or a bare JSON string.

descriptor.description: FTS-indexed at weight D (convention v1). The full section descriptor, for corpora where importers truncate it out of the title under length pressure — keeps the discriminating words searchable. A JSON string.

date.description: Shape-convention (not FTS-indexed). ISO-8601 calendar date, YYYY-MM-DD. The most common open_meta key in production.

relates_to.description: Shape-convention (not indexed). Soft relationship to other resources (UUIDs, slugs, or refs). Parallel to the hard edge model.

derived_from.description: Shape-convention (not indexed). Source resources this was derived from.

preceded_by.description: Shape-convention (not indexed). Resources that precede this in sequence.

references.description: Shape-convention (not indexed). Referenced resources or external URIs.

depends_on.description: Shape-convention (not indexed). Dependencies (UUIDs, slugs, or refs).
